meta.title: PC Health Check — A local diagnostic for the PC that feels busy behind your back
meta.description: A local diagnostic that explains hidden-miner suspicion, Korean banking-plugin false positives, and macOS's vague System Data / Developer storage categories in context. PC Health Check ships as Windows Edition and Mac Edition, with traffic-light reports and hash-only VirusTotal lookup.

hero.title: A local diagnostic for the PC that feels busy behind your back.
hero.subtitle: When fans spin, CPU/GPU spikes, or unknown processes appear, PC Health Check gathers miner signals, autoruns, network context, and Korean security-plugin explanations before you delete anything.

preview.accessible_summary: Example scan result: 3 items to review, 0 threats, and 93 safe items

problem.heading: When you wonder "is my PC secretly mining?", what should you check first?
problem.lead: A slow PC, spinning fans, high CPU/GPU, and unknown startup entries create real anxiety. A Korean PC also carries banking, government, and e-commerce security plugins that often look suspicious. PC Health Check gathers evidence before you ignore everything or delete the wrong thing.

how.heading: 8 diagnostics → 1 HTML report.
how.lead: No tracking installer. OS-specific scripts collect system state, then Windows assembles an HTML report while Mac uses a system-component SwiftUI utility plus HTML export.

p1.title: Collect
p1.desc: Processes, network, ports, autoruns, security posture. Sysinternals + launchd + VirusTotal hash lookup.

p2.title: Normalize
p2.desc: Match against Korean software whitelist, assign traffic-light verdict. OS-agnostic schema.

p3.title: Render
p3.desc: Windows gets a single HTML report. Mac gets a native SwiftUI utility plus HTML export. No tracking installer or report upload.

privacy.heading: Don't take "we're safe" on faith — read the code.
privacy.lead: Every network call is in the source. VirusTotal lookups send only the SHA-256 hash; file contents never leave your machine. Here's the actual call site.

hash.title: Hash only, no file upload
hash.desc: Get-FileHash computes SHA-256 locally. The GET request carries just the hash. If the hash is unknown, we return "unknown" — we never upload the file.

telemetry.title: Zero telemetry
telemetry.desc: No usage stats, no error reports, nothing sent to us. Diagnostic runtime outbound calls are limited to opt-in VirusTotal lookups and optional Sysinternals downloads.

local.title: Local cache only
local.desc: VT responses cache in %LOCALAPPDATA% (Windows) or ~/Library/Caches (macOS) for 48h. No cloud account, no sync.

audit.title: Source you can read
audit.desc: Readable PowerShell · Bash · JXA source. Release zips do not bundle prebuilt diagnostic binaries. grep -R 'Invoke-RestMethod\|Invoke-WebRequest\|curl\|virustotal.com/api' scripts/ finds outbound calls.

download.heading: Review and build the source.
download.lead: No public installer is available yet. Run the reviewed source, or wait for a release with signing, notarization, Gatekeeper, and SHA-256 metadata.

windows.title: Windows Edition
windows.desc: Windows 10 / 11 · PowerShell 5.1. Review the source, then run 검사하기.bat.

macos.title: Mac Edition
macos.desc: macOS 13+ SwiftUI app via Mac앱실행.command; script mode via 검사하기.command → Open. Decoding the System Data / Developer storage bar is a Mac Edition feature.

download.github_side: Clone or fork: github.com/heznpc/pc-health-check. Inspect the readable source yourself.

final.heading: Suspicious right now? 6 minutes to answers.
final.lead: No sign-up. No email. The report opens in your browser, and only your browser.

footer.credits: Thanks to Microsoft Sysinternals · Objective-See · the Malware Zero community.
